prompts.text: Vocabulary to introduce before starting: 'cashier' (person at the till), 'customer' (person shopping), 'item', 'price', 'receipt'. 'You're the customer — I'm the cashier. Customers choose; cashiers scan.'
Use sequencing language throughout: 'first', 'then', 'next', 'finally'. 'First we choose our list. Then we find everything. Finally we pay.' The child should hear and begin to echo this structure.
Core counting question: 'How many items are on the list? How many have we found? How many left?' — counting with purpose in a real-world context.
Role-reversal: 'You be the cashier now — what do you say to the customer?' — shifts perspective and practises community vocabulary from both sides.
